zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth de Quincy於1220年出生在英國哈普爾維古姆時，她的父親Roger de Quincy 2nd Earl of Winchester25歲，母親Helen de Galloway20歲。她於1240年2月與Sir Hugh de Neville結婚。她去世於大約1303年5月4日在蘇格蘭阿爾迪史爾布臣,第一代特威茲穆爾男爵享年83歲，埋葬在蘇格蘭班福爾班福。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Quincy
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): habitational name from any of several places in France deriving their names from the Gallo-Roman personal name Quintus, meaning ‘fifth(-born)’ + the locative suffix -acum. The earliest bearer of the name in England, that of Saer de Quincy, ancestor of the earls of Winchester, was from Cuinchy in Pas-de-Calais, but the name may have been reinforced by immigrants from Quincy-sous-Sénard in Seine-et-Oise or Quincy-Voisins in Seine-et-Marne. A family of the name de Quincy held the title Earl of Winchester 1207–65. Descendants of this family later bore the name Quincey up to the time of the English essayist Thomas De Quincey (1785–1859), who changed his name from Quincey to De Quincey in 1796.

History: The American Quincy family were established in MA by Edmund Quincy in 1633. Fifth in descent was Josiah Quincy (1744–75), a leading patriot, who was sent to England to argue the colonists’ case in 1774. His son Josiah (1772–1864) was a powerful opponent of slavery, president of Harvard, and mayor of Boston, a post also held by several of his descendants. The traditional pronunciation is “Quinzy”.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth de Quincy was born in 1220, in Winchester, Hampshire, England, her father, Roger de Quincy 2nd Earl of Winchester, was 25 and her mother, Helen de Galloway, was 20. She married Sir Hugh de Neville in February 1240. She died about 4 May 1303, in Buchan, Aberdeenshire, Scotland, at the age of 83, and was buried in Banff, Banffshire, Scotland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabeth de Quincy est née en 1220, à Winchester, Hampshire, Angleterre, son père, Roger de Quincy 2nd Earl of Winchester, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Helen de Galloway, avait 20 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Hugh de Neville en février 1240. Elle est décédée environ 4 mai 1303, à Buchan, Aberdeenshire, Écosse, à l’âge de 83 ans, et a été enterrée à Banff, Banffshire, Écosse.

Sie hat im Februar 1240 Sir Hugh de Neville geheiratet. Sie starb um 4. Mai 1303 im Alter von 83 Jahren in Buchan, Aberdeenshire, Scotland und wurde in Banff, Banffshire, Scotland begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth de Quincyが1220年にイギリスハンプシュアイアウィンチェスターで生まれた時，父Roger de Quincy 2nd Earl of Winchesterは25歳，母Helen de Gallowayは20歳であった。1240年2月にSir Hugh de Nevilleと結婚した。彼女は約1303年5月4日にスコットランドアバーディーンシュアイアブチャンで83歳で死去し，スコットランドバンフシュアイアバンッフにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth de Quincy(이)가 1220년 영국 함프실르 윈체스터에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Roger de Quincy 2nd Earl of Winchester(은)는 25세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Helen de Galloway(은)는 20세였다. 그녀는 1240년 2월 Sir Hugh de Neville(와)과 결혼했다. 그녀는 대략 1303년 5월 4일 스컷랜드 압르디실르 버챈에서 83세의 나이로 사망했으며 스컷랜드 반프실르 반프에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth de Quincy nasceu em 1220, em Winchester, Hampshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Roger de Quincy 2nd Earl of Winchester, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Helen de Galloway, tinha 20 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir Hugh de Neville em fevereiro de 1240. Ela faleceu aproximadamente 4 de maio de 1303, em Buchan, Aberdeenshire, Scotland, com 83 anos, e foi sepultada em Banff, Banffshire, Scotland.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth de Quincy родилась в 1220 г. в местности винчестир, хампшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Roger de Quincy 2nd Earl of Winchester было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Helen de Galloway, 20. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Hugh de Neville февраль 1240 г. Она умерла около 4 мая 1303 г. в местности бучань, абирдиншир, Шотландия в возрасте 83 лет и была похоронена в местности банфф, банффшир, Шотландия.

Se casó con Sir Hugh de Neville en febrero de 1240. Murió aproximadamente 4 de mayo de 1303, en Buchan, Aberdeenshire, Scotland, a la edad de 83 años, y fue sepultada en Banff, Banffshire, Scotland.
